pages.raw_text:
This is the ultimatum served up
by the people interested in the
fight to prevent Union park being
invaded by saloon. AS they con-
troll 1900 or more votes in that
ward the outcome looks dark po-
litically for the councilmen that
pay more heed to the brewers than
to their constituents.


The temperance people don't
mean to resort to threat to force
action they favor, but they reason
that their councilmen are bound by
the wishes and will of the majority
and are determined to emphasize
that position.


The attitude of Union park will
not affect Councilman Brown much,
as he is out already, but it comes
close to Funk, who has never dem
onstrated any intention to commit
political suicide. Since the council
raised its own pay he probably likes
the job better than ever.


But it is understood around the
city hall that Funk would favor
the license being granted If he
dares to.


The indications are that the
